front: Author: protagonist Saleem Sinai is born at the exact moment of India's independence with telepathic powers from his large nose
Author: Ayatollah Khomeini issued a fatwa calling for this author's death in 1989
Author: Saladin Chamcha and Gibreel Farishta survive falling from a hijacked plane over the English Channel
Author: lived in hiding for a decade under the pseudonym 'Joseph Anton,' derived from the first names of Conrad and Chekhov
Author: nurse Mary Pereira swaps two babies, Saleem and Shiva of the Knees, at birth
Author: 'the Widow' represents Indira Gandhi, who sued this author over his novel
Author: Parvati-the-Witch helps the protagonist before the destruction of a slum
Author: Ayesha leads pilgrims into the Arabian Sea, where they drown, in a dream sequence
Author: essay 'Commonwealth Literature Does Not Exist' attacks the 'bogy of Authenticity'
Author: essay 'Outside the Whale' calls for 'rowdyism' against Orwell's quietism in 'Inside the Whale'
Author: memoir describes marriages to Marianne Wiggins and Padma Lakshmi (called 'The Illusion')
Author: Japanese translator Hitoshi Igarashi was killed in 1991; Ettore Capriolo and William Nygaard were also injured
Author: Khattam-Shud tries to poison the Sea of Stories and silence the residents of Gup on the moon Kahani
back: Salman Rushdie (Haroun and the Sea of Stories)
work: Haroun and the Sea of Stories

front: Author: Iskander Harappa and General Raza Hyder are politicians in an allegorical version of Pakistan
Author: first novel about the Axona Indian Flapping Eagle who searches for sister Bird-Dog for 777 years
Author: appeared at a U2 Wembley concert in 1993, telling Bono 'real devils don't wear horns'
Author: Gibreel Farishta, inspired by Amitabh Bachchan, has a relationship with mountaineer Allie Cone
Author: title essay discusses straddling two cultures and 'the migrant theory'; declares 'we are translated men'
Author: Nero and his children leave Mumbai for New York in a 2017 novel; filmmaker Rene uses 'The Joker' for Trump
Author: Ormus Cana and Vina Apsara become 20th-century Orpheus and Eurydice analogues
back: Salman Rushdie (The Ground Beneath Her Feet)

front: Author: stabbed in 2022 by Hadi Matar at a Chautauqua lecture; wrote the memoir Knife about the attack
Author: wrote a monograph on The Wizard of Oz, comparing it to 'the authorless text'; the film inspired his writing career
Author: won the 1995 Whitbread Prize for best novel for The Moor's Last Sigh
Author: a chauffeur-turned-assassin from Kashmir is nicknamed 'the clown'
Author: story collection includes agents named Chekhov and Zulu and 'The Prophet's Hair'
Author: protagonist of his 2015 novel is a princess who controls lightning, set in New York, alluding to 1001 Nights
back: Salman Rushdie (Two Years Eight Months and Twenty-Eight Nights)
work: Two Years Eight Months and Twenty-Eight Nights

front: Author: his country is described as a 'collective fiction' and 'new myth'; protagonist flees into the jungle after being hit by a silver spittoon
Author: goddess allows Pampa Kampana to conjure up the city of Bisnaga in Victory City
Author: first nonfiction book, The Jaguar Smile, about his trip to Nicaragua after being invited by the Sandinistas
